reviews.review_text: This is probably the best campground in the snowy mountains the toilets are clean and the views are stunning lots of bike tracks around Thredbo river is so clean and there’s a platypus that hangs around however the deer are a big problem

Very popular with families with kids. Camping spots are tight.
Very scenic.

Pretty clean and open during March.

Close to the river.

Lots of bugs and wombats

It's a really lovely campsite.
There's not a hape of available sites.
As the population grows, it'll be nice to see some other areas that are suitable for campgrounds opened up.
Campground has a lovely family feeling about it. Kids learning to fish, fantastic swimming hauls, campfires the push bike trail and not too far away from thredbo if you need something from the supermarket.
Great campground, thanks for NP Operator maintaining such beautiful spot

Camped here during our Kosci 30 run and had a blast. Great amenities and local wildlife. We had site 22 (I think sites have changed since we stayed) and we just fit our entire set up. Beautiful view of the river and proximity to parking, toilets and covered picnic table. It was suuuuuper windy so definitely come prepared for that - bloke next to us was sleeping inside his swag and the entire thing flipped!
Great camp ground very neat and tidy.
Very nice, quiet place.
This place is superb.
Beautiful camp ground with a creek.

Thredbo Diggings is a beautifully kept NPWS camping ground. It's located right on the Thredbo River, which is ideal for a cool down after a day of hiking or mountain biking the many trails in Kosciuszko National Park. It's close to Thredbo Village, 20 mins to Jindabyne and nearly an hour to Charlotte Pass where a number of hiking/bike tracks start. At night the sky fills up with up a carpet of stars in a show that's breathtaking. We stayed for two weeks in Feb/March and loved the tranquillity and feel of Thredbo Diggings.
Quite a large and well used pretty campground

The Diggings is a fantastic location, and the camping area is pristine, too. It is a great spot to bring the kids. They can ride their bike and play with other kids on the site. It has bikes and walking track available. Moving water to go fishing 🎣. There is a shared public toilet, very handy for people who don't want to rough it too much. Make sure you get national parks pass before you head to the camping ground.
This place is heaven. The most basic of facilities, but who needs that stuff when in a place like this. Get a site on the river front if you can.
It's a great spot to camp next to Little Thredbo River. Well suited to families with kids.
Beautiful and peaceful place to stay. Highly recommend this spot, definitely will be back again
Great place to set up camp. Small lake nearby
Most spots fit 22ft caravans , lots of wild life  and very gorgeous just to sit by the river.

The place is very awesome for camping. However, If you are not prepared enough then, this place may give some bad nights who are doing camping for the first time or living in a tent. We camped in this place in peak winter and we faced blizzard all night. We only lived in our tent and it was almost impossible to go outside. Similarly, the weather was so bad that we couldnot withstand the wind and snow, we had to spend the night in our car. If you are planning to stay overnight and thinking about cooking your food on the firepit then its impossible because of the wind and snow. Better bring stove and butane gas. Mornings are chill in this place because the weather was calm and peaceful. We also got chance to see some kangaroos and native deers. My suggestion is be very prepared before camping in this site.
My overall honest reviews are
Location 6/5
Facilities (1/5 no drinking water and electricity)
Will to go again 10/5
Weather 2/5 depends on days.
We paid 6 aud for the location and 29 for parking.
Place to relax and enjoy the nature, we camped just next to river, not luck enough to catch any fish. Plenty of wood. Great experience

Nice back to nature campground. Busy even in summer . Bushwalk or bike ride.
Friendly mallard ducks, shy woodland ducks ,  intrusive currawong birds( lock your food up). By night  cheeky possums, deer.
Camping near the river, fishing and freezing cold swimming.
Campsite has 3 long drop toilets that are clean.
No showers ,power  or tap water so be prepared to provide your own.
Campsites generously sized and mostly flat  except for sites 5,6 and 7.
Road in is steep  2 way and only wide enough for 1 vehicle , so be cautious if you are towing.

A beautiful quiet little campground right by the Snowy River. Most sites have a fire pit with some also having a grill plate on the fire pit.
There are two drop toilets that were kept clean during my visit. No showers or other amenities. Close to both Jindabyne and Thredbo.
Wahrscheinlich ein ganz typisches Bushcamp. Ohne WC, ohne Wasser, Ohne Strom. Bush-Toilette vorhanden.
EPIC campground. Amazing scenery, wildlife and the perfect place 👌 The nights sky was absolutely amazing.

The campsite was great, although the weather was challenging.
Which is part of the adventure when camping
Nice spot, we didn't stay there just checking it out. Right on the river
Campground has swimming stream nearby. Long drop toilet is up the hill. Sites are well marked and a good enough size, only issue is that you have to walk through other people's site to get to yours.

Beautiful campground with fire pits and picnic tables. Long drop toilets and no showers.

The way that the campsites are laid out will guarantee that people are walking through your site - or your are walking through theirs. Being in site #38 we had to walk through sites to get to the parking area.
Why does nswp still insists on COVID  bookings? Restrictions had been removed a year ago.

Arrived early afternoon, plenty of fire wood to collect from nearby shrub to create the perfect fire in the provided pits. On the note make sure you book sites with a fire pit symbol otherwise you’ll be stitched up watching your neighbours enjoying theirs. I feel the spots are specious enough but a push for larger caravans (we have a rooftop tent&awning set up) possums are friendly and will climb on car & equipment but aren’t too invasive…. Yet 😂otherwise lovely spot with river and snowy mountains views
Beware!! Generator NOISE all night from caravans running heaters!!!! Booking system sucks too! Will never stay here again
出乎意料地漂亮
Right on the Thredbo river
Beautiful place to camp, surrounded by mountains right next to the river.
